prompts.reason: Both show many cubes on a ramp with reset/contact overlays and browser routes. Aura uses much less code; Three.js has a broader ramp view. Visual/prompt result is close.
Both render dense particle fountains with controls/HUD. Aura is concise and visibly dense; Three.js is also visually strong.
Aura renders a sun and planets with labels. Claude/Three.js timed out and captured only scaffold text, so it fails visual and coverage requirements.
Aura renders a neon tunnel/flythrough view. Claude/Three.js timed out and captured only scaffold text, so it fails visual and coverage requirements.
Both render readable 3D bar charts. Three.js has stronger axis labels, Aura is more concise with adequate prompt coverage.
Aura shows a simple mini-golf scene but interaction/evidence is minimal. Three.js has a more complete putting board, ball, obstacle, flag, and HUD.
Aura material swatches are visible but cropped and material distinctions are less complete. Three.js shows a fuller row with clearer glass/metal/material variety.
Aura renders a usable city block with toggle. Three.js shows a larger, richer city with stronger streets/windows/scale variation.
Aura humanoid is visible but very primitive and low-detail. Three.js reads more like an animated character in a world space.
Both load the provided sneaker on a plinth with studio lighting and orbit/turntable intent. Visual result is close.

summary.reason: Claude/Aura improves process reliability and wins prompts where Claude/Three.js timed out, but it reaches only 2 wins with several visual-quality losses on mini-golf, material lab, city, and humanoid.
